target.name: OpenAI
target.founder: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $7.0B - $183.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Anthropic
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $183B

private.name: Anysphere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $29.3B

private.name: Neuralink
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $9.7B

private.name: Groq
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $7B

data.narrative:
OpenAI, a private-stage AI & Software company, has raised $13B+ and is currently valued at $300B+. Our valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with direct peer medians derived from 4 same-sector, same-stage companies: Anthropic ($183.0B), Anysphere ($29.3B), Neuralink ($9.7B), and Groq ($7.0B).

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $19.5B based on the median of the four-company sample. This method captures a range from $7.0B (Groq floor) to $183.0B (Anthropic ceiling), reflecting material heterogeneity within the AI frontier-tech cohort.

Our triangulated valuation supports a range of $16.6B to $22.4B, centered on $19.5B. This analysis is anchored to a single methodological input and therefore carries material uncertainty. The wide dispersion among peer valuations—a 26x spread from low to high—underscores that comparable-company analysis alone provides limited resolution in an early-stage, high-variance sector. The current stated valuation of $300B+ substantially exceeds peer-derived estimates and would require supportive revenue, growth, or market-position data not present in this dataset to justify through fundamental methods.
